name: Tiramisu Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: Cream cheese (24 oz) or Neufchatel cheese Room t
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Butter an 8-inch springform pan and stir in butter and espresso powder in a small bowl until combined. Stir in crumbs until evenly moistened. Pat over the bottom of the prepared pan and bake for 10 minutes. Cool on a wire rack. Keep the oven on.
3. Tightly cover the outside bottom and sides of the springform pan with heavy-duty foil.
4. Meanwhile, beat cream cheese and mascarpone in a large mixer bowl at medium-high speed until light and fluffy (2 minutes). Gradually beat in sugar until completely smooth (3 minutes), scraping down the sides of the bowl with a rubber spatula.
5. Reduce speed to medium and beat in vanilla and salt. Add eggs, one at a time, beating just until blended after each addition.
6. Pour the filling over the crust in the prepared pan and place the pan in a large roasting pan. Dissolve espresso in hot water. Fold into the remaining filling with brandy and grated chocolate.
7. Spoon the mixture evenly over the filling in the pan and smooth the top with a spatula. Place the pan on the oven rack. Pour enough hot water into the roasting pan to come 1 inch up the side of the springform pan.
8. Bake for 1-1/4 hours or until the center is just set. Remove the cheesecake from the water bath and let it cool completely on a wire rack. Remove the foil, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate overnight.
9. Just before serving, run a knife around the edge of the pan, remove the sides, sprinkle lightly with cocoa, and serve.
Makes 12 servings.
